content: COVID-19 diagnosis: 1) NAAT/PCR (gold standard, highest sensitivity, detects viral RNA); 2) Rapid antigen tests (lower sensitivity, best for symptomatic patients within first week, serial testing improves sensitivity); 3) Antibody tests indicate prior infection/vaccination but not acute diagnosis. Testing indicated for symptomatic individuals, post-exposure screening, pre-procedure requirements. Isolation guidance: stay home until fever-free 24 hours without antipyretics and symptoms improving; wear mask through day 10. Severity classification: mild (no hypoxia), moderate (oxygen saturation ≥90% on room air), severe (<90% on room air, respiratory rate >30, lung infiltrates >50%), critical (respiratory failure, septic shock, organ dysfunction).
